title: Stop-Cluster Cascade Exhaustion
focus_questions: Did the stop cascade become accepted outside trade, or only an exhaustion burst?
After the cascade, did the first retest hold or fail?

summary.thesis: A stop cascade can create impressive extension without creating durable new value, so the real edge comes from reading the post-cascade branch.
summary.operator_translation: Do not chase the stop release itself; wait to see whether the cascade rebuilt or exhausted.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card when a visible stop area is breached and the resulting release looks powerful enough to tempt immediate continuation assumptions.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: a stop cluster is likely triggered and price extends quickly. Required evidence: cascade release, outside hold quality, and the first meaningful retest after the release. Do not trade: by chasing the first cascade or by fading it before hold quality fails. Real trigger: accepted outside trade after the retest or clear cascade exhaustion after rebuild fails. Failure point: the market proves the opposite branch on the first readable retest.

when_relevant: A likely stop area is breached and price accelerates sharply.
The extension looks strong enough to invite late chasing or emotional fading.
The operator needs to know whether the cascade became accepted trade or only stop fuel.
required_evidence: a plausible stop cluster was actually triggered
the release through the stop area can be separated from the retest after it
post-cascade outside hold quality can be judged
confirmation_signals: the cascade releases quickly once the stop area is breached
outside trade either stays accepted or loses hold quality on the first meaningful retest
the post-cascade branch becomes clearer than the initial burst
invalidation_signals: there is no meaningful stop area and the burst is being over-labeled
outside hold quality is still unresolved and the card is being forced too early
the market clearly proves the opposite post-cascade branch
no_trade_conditions: Do not chase the first stop cascade before outside acceptance is visible.
Do not fade the cascade immediately before outside hold quality actually fails.
Do not assume that a violent extension automatically created new accepted value.

entry_archetypes.name: post-cascade hold and continue
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the cascade retest holds and outside trade proves accepted.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the outside hold is still thin and unresolved.

entry_archetypes.name: cascade exhaustion reversion
entry_archetypes.description: Enter after the cascade fails to rebuild and the market starts repairing back toward old auction.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the cascade still holds outside and rebuilds cleanly.

management_notes: Stop fuel can create distance faster than true initiative can create structure.
The best continuation usually comes after the first retest holds.
If the cascade cannot rebuild after release, the move often slips into repair.
review_questions: Was the operator trading stop fuel or accepted continuation?
After the cascade, did outside trade hold or fail?
Where did the market prove exhaustion or prove rebuild?
